blurb:
This is hands down the best Mexican food in Sandy Springs, and I'll fight anyone who says otherwise. The vibe is fun and colorful, great for groups or casual dates, and they're open until midnight most nights which is clutch when you get a late taco craving.

The Esquite is what everyone talks about and it's worth the hype. Their tacos are legit, but don't sleep on the drink menu. The Pineapple Guava pitcher and Passion Fruit pitcher are both dangerous in the best way. Everything tastes fresh and made to order, not like it's been sitting under a heat lamp.

Service is fast and friendly, and the place has energy. Perfect for after work drinks or weekend hangouts with friends.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This food hall right on the square is perfect when your family can't agree on what to eat. Twenty different vendors means everyone gets what they want and you can all sit together in the shared seating area. Great for lunch downtown or after walking around the square.

The variety is impressive and changes somewhat regularly. You'll find everything from tacos to Asian food to good old Southern comfort food. It's also nice for trying new things without committing to a full restaurant meal.

Parking can be tricky during busy times on the square, but it's worth it for the convenience. Perfect for a casual meal when you're already downtown for shopping or events.
address: 68 North Marietta Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30060, USA
hours: Mon-Thu 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Fri-Sat 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Sun 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM

blurb:
If you've never tried Brazilian all-you-can-eat, this is a great place to start. They bring grilled meats right to your table on skewers and you eat until you can't anymore. It's fun for special occasions or when you want to try something different with friends.

The cheese bread alone is worth the trip and the barbecue is really flavorful. They also have feijoada stew which is their traditional black bean dish if you want something besides just meat. The salad bar helps balance out all that protein too.

It's in the Powers Ferry shopping center and the atmosphere is relaxed. Good for birthdays or date nights when you want to make it an experience rather than just grabbing a quick bite.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
hours: Mon-Thu 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Fri-Sat 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Sun 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
This little Middle Eastern spot near Franklin Gateway has become my go-to when I want something healthy and flavorful without spending a fortune. The counter-service setup keeps things casual and quick, perfect for a lunch break or easy dinner.

Their shawarma is what most people come for and it's really good. I also love the falafel which comes out crispy and hot. The portions are generous and everything tastes fresh. They have plenty of veggie options too if you're trying to eat lighter.

It's in a strip mall but don't let that fool you. The food is authentic and the prices are very reasonable for the amount of food you get.
address: 1175 Franklin Gateway SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb:
My wife discovered this place for girls' night and now we're regulars. It's Persian food done right with a modern vibe that feels special enough for celebrations but comfortable for regular dinners. The cocktail menu is surprisingly good, and the whole experience feels like something you'd find in a bigger city.

Start with the hummus and wings, which sounds weird but trust me on this one. The chicken shish kabobs with the rice that has lentils and raisins is what I order every time. Everything is flavorful without being heavy, and portions are generous. Save room for the tiramisu because it's not your typical version.

They break between lunch and dinner, so check the hours before you go. Definitely worth making a reservation for weekend dinners because this place gets packed.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This authentic French bakery feels like a little slice of Paris tucked away on South Marietta Parkway. The owner is actually French and you can taste the difference in everything they make. Perfect for weekend brunch or picking up pastries for a special occasion.

Their croissants are buttery and flaky like they should be, and the quiches make a great lunch. The petit fours are beautiful if you need something fancy for a party. Everything is made fresh daily and they often sell out of popular items.

Get there early on weekends because the best stuff goes fast. The Sunday hours are shorter too, so plan accordingly. It's become our go-to spot when we want to feel a little fancy without the fuss.
address: 277 South Marietta Pkwy SW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA
hours: Mon-Sat 7:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Sun 9:00 AM – 3:30 PM

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb:
This place has ruined regular pizza for me. It's Neapolitan style with a thin crust that's somehow both crispy and chewy, and they use ingredients that actually taste like something. Good for date night or when you want pizza that feels a little special but isn't pretentious about it.

The Margherita is perfect if you want to taste what they're really about, but I usually go for one of their specialty pizzas. Everything is made fresh and comes out of a proper wood-fired oven. They also do salads and antipasti that are actually worth ordering, not just afterthoughts.

It gets busy on weekends, so either come early or be prepared to wait a bit. The space is nice inside, not just another strip mall pizza joint.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This is my go-to spot when I'm craving proper Indian food and want family-style portions that'll feed everyone. It's in the Perimeter Center area, nothing fancy looking, but the food is legit and they don't mess around with tiny portions. Perfect for feeding a crowd or when you want leftovers for days.

The biryani is obviously what they're known for, and it's worth the hype. We usually get the chicken biryani and whatever curry catches our eye. The spice levels are real, so don't be a hero if you can't handle heat. Everything comes out hot and fresh, and you'll definitely have enough to take home.

Just know they break between lunch and dinner most days, so don't show up at 3pm expecting to eat. Weekend hours are longer if you want a more flexible schedule.
address: 130 Perimeter Center Pl, Dunwoody, GA 30346, USA

blurb:
This is the diner that never sleeps, literally. Open 24/7 right on Cobb Parkway, it's a Marietta institution with that classic neon sign you can spot from the road. Perfect for late-night cravings, early morning coffee runs, or when you need breakfast at 3pm because kids.

The menu is huge and includes Greek dishes alongside all the usual diner fare. Their pancakes are the size of dinner plates and the Greek omelet is what I order when I want something different from regular bacon and eggs. The portions are generous enough that you'll probably have leftovers.

Just know it gets busy on weekend mornings and the parking lot can be a bit chaotic. But when you need good comfort food at any hour of the day, this place delivers.
address: 306 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

blurb:
This is where we take my parents when they visit or celebrate anniversaries. It sits right on the Chattahoochee with huge windows overlooking the water, so you're paying for the view but it's actually worth it. The kind of place where you dress up a little and don't rush through dinner.

The seafood is what they do best, and portions are generous for a fancy place. We usually split an appetizer and still leave full. The Sunday brunch is popular if you want the experience without the full dinner price tag. Service is professional without being stuffy.

Reservations are pretty much required, especially for weekend dinners. Parking can be a pain when they're busy, so give yourself extra time.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA
